title: Youth in Evaluation Forum 2026: Global Digital Event Empowering Young Evaluators Worldwide
summary: The Youth in Evaluation Forum 2026 is set to bring together young and experienced evaluators from across the world for a dynamic three-day digital event. This global convening aims to amplify youth voices and reshape how evaluation responds to today s rapidly evolving social, economic, and environmental challenges. Through inclusive and inter-generational dialogue, the forum will celebrate innovation, encourage collaboration, and strengthen a future-ready evaluation community that values youth participation and leadership. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Why the Youth in Evaluation Forum Matters Evaluation plays a critical role in improving policies, programs, and development outcomes worldwide. By actively involving young people in evaluation, the global community can harness fresh perspectives, innovative ideas, and future-focused approaches that strengthen evidence-based decision-making. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The Youth in Evaluation Forum 2026 serves as a platform to bridge generational perspectives, empower emerging evaluators, and ensure that youth voices help shape the future of evaluation practice. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Deadline: 21 March 2026 (Expression of Interest for regional events) Event Dates: 19 21 May 2026 Event Dates to Remember Participants are invited to mark their calendars for the following key sessions: May 19, 2026 Global Inauguration and Champion Awards The forum will kick off with a high-level opening session highlighting youth leadership in evaluation. During this session, the 2026 Youth in Evaluation Champions will be officially announced and recognised for their contributions to advancing evaluation practice and youth engagement globally. May 20 21, 2026 Regional Events The following two days will feature interactive, partner-led regional sessions designed to address local priorities and challenges. These discussions will allow participants to exchange ideas, showcase innovations, and collaborate on strengthening evaluation ecosystems within their regions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Opportunities to Get Involved The Youth in Evaluation Forum encourages broad participation from individuals, organisation, and evaluation partners worldwide. Here are a few ways you can contribute: Host a Regional Event Organisations and partners are invited to organise regional sessions during the forum. Deadline to submit an Expression of Interest: 21 March 2026 Join the Conversation Stay updated on upcoming regional sessions and forum announcements by subscribing to the #Eval4Action Newsletter . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Commit to Action Show your support for youth inclusion in evaluation by signing the Youth in Evaluation Manifesto , which promotes meaningful engagement of young people in evaluation processes. The manifesto is currently available in 21 languages , making it accessible to a global audience. Click here, for more details about the Forum . Discover more similar opportunities Here. title: Applications Open for Aspen Kyiv–UK Leadership Bridge 2026 Leadership Fellowship (Deadline: 12 April 2026)
summary: The Aspen Institute Kyiv and Aspen Initiative UK have launched the Aspen Kyiv UK Leadership Bridge 2026 , an exclusive leadership fellowship designed to connect emerging leaders from the United Kingdom and Ukraine who are working at the intersection of democracy, security, and technology. In an era marked by geopolitical tension, democratic renewal, and rapid technological change, this fellowship aims to bring together 20 rising leaders (10 from the UK and 10 from Ukraine) to explore how leadership and democratic values must evolve. Through immersive seminars and collaborative projects, participants will strengthen their ethical leadership, strategic thinking, and cross-cultural collaboration skills to help address some of Europe s most pressing challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Program Timeline The fellowship includes three key learning engagements combining in-person and virtual experiences: UK Seminar: 21 25 June 2026 at the historic Madingley Hall , concluding with a day of high-level visits in London . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Online Seminar: 17 September 2026 Ukraine Seminar: 17 21 October 2026 in the Ukrainian Carpathian Mountains , hosted in partnership with Aspen Institute Kyiv . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); All seminars will be moderated by faculty members from Aspen UK and Aspen Kyiv, using Aspen s renowned values-based seminar method . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Collaborative Leadership Project During the fellowship, participants will work together to design cross-border initiatives addressing shared challenges between the UK and Ukraine. These projects may include: Policy proposals Trade and economic cooperation strategies Knowledge-sharing platforms Cross-sector partnerships Participants will present their initiatives at the end of the programme. Alumni Network and Engagement After completing the fellowship, participants will become part of the global Aspen leadership community through the networks of Aspen Initiative UK and Aspen Institute Kyiv . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Fellows will gain access to: Exclusive leadership events Continued cross-cohort collaboration Global opportunities for dialogue and partnership Who Should Apply? The fellowship is open to UK- and Ukraine-based emerging leaders who: Work in one of the following sectors: Public Sector (Government, Parliament, Civil Service) Security Defence Private Sector Media Civil Society or Academia Are aged 30 40 Have 5 15 years of professional experience Demonstrate interest in UK Ukraine relations Are eager to collaborate across sectors and borders to shape a more interconnected and democratic world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Fees and Logistics The programme is heavily subsidised by the Aspen Global Fund , but selected fellows will contribute: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 2,750 participation fee Travel to Cambridge for the UK seminar Visa costs (if required) International return flights to Ukraine The participation fee covers: Seminar materials and activities 4 nights accommodation, meals, and visits during the UK seminar 4 nights accommodation, meals, and cultural events during the Ukraine seminar Group transfers for the Ukraine seminar Limited scholarships may be available for applicants from the public sector, media, or civil society who require financial assistance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Why Apply? Selected fellows will: Build a lifelong global network of changemakers across the UK and Ukraine Engage with leading thinkers and experts in democracy, security, and technology Gain a platform to influence policy and leadership practice across Europe Develop as reflective, values-driven leaders through Aspen s seminar-based learning model Application Deadline Deadline: 12 April 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to apply through the official application link . Discover more similar fellowship Here . title: Apply Now: AfCFTA Sustainable Leap Youth Essay Competition 2026 (Win Recognition & Policy Opportunities)
summary: Young Africans passionate about sustainable development and trade policy now have an exciting opportunity to contribute their ideas to the future of continental trade. The AfCFTA Africa Umoja Campaign , convened by Young African Leadership and Development Agency (YALDA) and partners including African Continental Free Trade Area Secretariat , United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), International Trade Centre (ITC), United Nations Economic Commission for Africa (UNECA), and African Export-Import Bank (Afreximbank), has launched the Sustainable Leap Youth Essay Competition . The competition invites young Africans to present bold, policy-driven ideas on how the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) can drive sustainable growth, low-carbon manufacturing, and greener logistics systems across Africa . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Competition As Africa deepens regional integration through the AfCFTA, young thinkers and innovators are being called upon to help shape the continent s trade policies. This essay competition aims to spotlight youth-led ideas that promote sustainable trade practices, climate-friendly industrialization, and inclusive economic development across African markets. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Participants will have the opportunity to share innovative policy solutions that can help transform the AfCFTA into a powerful driver of Africa s green economic transition . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Why You Should Apply This competition is a unique platform for young Africans to influence policy conversations, showcase innovative thinking, and contribute to building a greener trade future for the continent . If you are passionate about sustainable development, economic integration, and climate-smart trade, this is your chance to make your voice heard. Submission Requirements Applicants must submit two components as part of their entry: 1. Essay A 2,000-word blog-style essay Must be well researched and focused on sustainable trade within the AfCFTA framework Submit in .docx (Word) format ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 2. Video Pitch A 1-minute (60-second) unedited video Should clearly summarize the essay s key idea and policy recommendation Upload the video to a publicly accessible platform (e.g., YouTube private link) and include the link in the submission form. REGISTER HERE TO APPLY. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Eligibility Criteria To participate, applicants must: Be an African passport holder Be between 18 and 35 years old Submit both the essay and video pitch before the deadline Benefits and Prizes Winners of the competition will receive: Continental recognition for their ideas High-value career-advancing incentives Exclusive invitations to high-level trade and policy events ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Additional prize details will be announced by the organizers. Application Deadline 22 March 2026 Discover more similar Opportunities here The post Apply Now: AfCFTA Sustainable Leap Youth Essay Competition 2026 (Win Recognition Policy Opportunities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Chevening British Library Fellowship (2027) for Mid-Career Researchers and Professionals
summary: The Chevening British Library Fellowship has officially been relaunched, offering mid-career experts the chance to spend 12 months working with the British Library in the United Kingdom. This fellowship is for researchers interested in cultural heritage, archives, and international collaboration and who would like the opportunity to work with one of the world s most renowned research libraries. The fellowship is jointly supported by Chevening and the UK Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) and aims to strengthen global research collaboration while bringing new perspectives to the British Library s collections and archives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Through this program, fellows will contribute research, develop resources that make collections more accessible, and help strengthen connections between the British Library and international communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Chevening British Library Fellowships The relaunch introduces two distinct fellowship opportunities , each offering one fellowship place for qualified applicants. These fellowships are designed for mid-career professionals, academics, and researchers who want to contribute their expertise while engaging with the British Library s extensive collections. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The fellowships provide a 12-month placement at the British Library , one of the world s largest research libraries with collections spanning centuries of global knowledge and cultural heritage. During the fellowship, participants will collaborate with curators, cataloguing specialists, and research staff to explore historical collections, conduct research projects, and develop outputs that help broaden access to these materials. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Why This Fellowship Matters The Chevening British Library Fellowship highlights the importance of international collaboration in preserving and understanding cultural heritage. By inviting researchers and professionals from around the world to work with its collections, the British Library aims to generate new insights, expand global research networks, and make historical materials more accessible. Both Chevening and the British Library emphasize the long-term impact of these fellowships, aiming to create a global community of researchers and cultural leaders who continue to collaborate beyond the fellowship period. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); For mid-career professionals interested in archives, cultural heritage, research, and international collaboration, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to contribute to one of the world s most important knowledge institutions. Fellowship Tracks Applicants can apply for one of the two available fellowships depending on their regional expertise and professional background. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 1. Chevening Southeast Europe British Library Fellowship This fellowship focuses on improving and expanding access to the British Library s collections related to Southeast Europe. It is open to professionals from countries including: Albania Bosnia and Herzegovina Kosovo Moldova Montenegro North Macedonia Serbia Turkey Fellows will work closely with the library s Southeast European collections team to enhance catalog records, analyse collection data, and produce research outputs that help make these materials more accessible to scholars and the public. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The fellowship may include activities such as improving catalog metadata, writing research articles or blog posts, and producing collection guides that highlight the region s intellectual and cultural heritage. 2. Chevening Overseas Territories in the Atlantic and Caribbean British Library Fellowship The second fellowship focuses on the representation of the British Overseas Territories in the Atlantic and Caribbean within the British Library s collections. This opportunity is open to professionals from territories such as: Anguilla Bermuda British Virgin Islands Cayman Islands Falkland Islands Montserrat St Helena Turks and Caicos Islands ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Fellows will work on projects aimed at redefining how the histories and cultural records of these territories are represented within the British Library. Activities may include creating collection guides, preparing presentations for researchers and the public, and producing written content that highlights the significance of these collections. What the Fellowship Covers The Chevening British Library Fellowship offers a comprehensive support package designed to enable fellows to focus on their research and professional contributions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Benefits include: Covered living expenses during the fellowship Return economy airfare to the UK Allowance for fellowship-related activities Up to 1,000 for approved project-related expenses This financial support ensures fellows can fully engage with their research and collaboration activities throughout the program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Fellowship Duration The fellowship lasts 12 months , allowing fellows to undertake in-depth research projects and contribute meaningfully to the British Library s work. The expected timeline is: Start date: January 2027 End date: December 2027 or January 2028 During this period, fellows will work alongside library specialists and participate in research and knowledge-exchange activities. Application Deadline Application Deadline: May 15, 2026 Applicants should review the eligibility requirements and prepare their application materials well in advance of the deadline. You can find these on the official Chevening Website. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Apply for the Chevening British Library Fellowship (2027) for Mid-Career Researchers and Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Fully Funded PhD on Environmental Change Agents in Hybrid and Remote Work Environments (UK)
summary: Researchers interested in sustainability, organizational change, and the future of work now have the opportunity to apply for a fully funded PhD studentship titled Environmental Change Agents in Hybrid and Remote Work Environments. The project will be hosted by Norwich Business School at the University of East Anglia (UEA) and is affiliated with the ESRC Centre for Climate Change and Social Transformations (CAST) . This interdisciplinary research opportunity will explore how digital workplaces influence coalition building and the spread of sustainability culture within organizations. As hybrid and remote working models become increasingly common, the project aims to examine how individuals within organizations can act as effective change agents to promote sustainable practices and behavioral transformation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The funded PhD is expected to begin in October 2026 and offers financial support alongside the chance to collaborate with leading researchers studying climate change, social transformation, and workplace innovation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Understanding the Research Focus Organizations play a crucial role in achieving global climate goals, yet turning sustainability commitments into everyday workplace practices remains a significant challenge. Many companies have adopted sustainability frameworks and environmental pledges, but implementing these policies effectively often requires cultural and behavioral change within the organization. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); This doctoral project seeks to understand how individuals inside organizations referred to as environmental change agents can help translate sustainability goals into meaningful workplace actions. The research will examine the internal and external factors that shape pro-environmental behavior among employees, as well as the strategies individuals use to encourage colleagues and stakeholders to support sustainability initiatives. A particular focus of the project is how digital tools and hybrid work environments affect collaboration and coalition building around sustainability efforts. With more organizations adopting flexible work arrangements, digital workplaces are becoming key spaces for communication, teamwork, and cultural development. Research suggests that hybrid work environments rely heavily on digital ecosystems that enable remote collaboration, task management, and communication across locations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The PhD will explore whether technologies such as digital platforms, green applications, or AI-enabled tools can help or hinder sustainability initiatives inside organizations. Why This Opportunity Matters As organizations increasingly adopt hybrid work models, understanding how workplace culture evolves in digital environments has become an important research area. Hybrid work arrangements blend remote and office-based work, creating new opportunities for collaboration but also raising questions about communication, organizational culture, and sustainability practices. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); By examining how sustainability initiatives spread within digital workplaces, this PhD project aims to generate insights that can help organizations build stronger environmental cultures and accelerate climate action. The research will contribute not only to academic knowledge but also to practical solutions that organizations can implement to support sustainable workplace transformation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Research Methods and Academic Environment The PhD project will combine a variety of research methods, allowing the candidate to develop strong analytical and methodological skills. The research may include: Literature reviews and desk research Qualitative interviews with employees and organizational stakeholders Survey-based data collection Comparative case studies across organizations ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Students will also have access to existing research data examining how employees interpret and respond to sustainability practices within their organizations. The project is part of the CAST research program , an international initiative dedicated to understanding and accelerating social transformations related to climate change. CAST s work focuses on difficult behavioral challenges associated with areas such as food systems, transportation, and energy consumption. Through this affiliation, the successful candidate will join an interdisciplinary network of researchers studying how societies transition toward more sustainable systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Funding and Benefits This opportunity is offered as a fully funded three-year ESRC CAST PhD studentship . The funding package includes: Home tuition fees covered A tax-free annual stipend of 21,805 for the 2026 2027 academic year Access to research resources and academic supervision at the University of East Anglia The position is based in Norwich, United Kingdom , within the Norwich Business School research community. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Eligibility Requirements Applicants should meet the following requirements: A first-class or upper second-class undergraduate degree (2:1 or equivalent) in fields such as Business, Management, Sociology, Psychology, Political Science, or environmental social sciences A Master s degree in a relevant subject area Demonstrated interest in topics such as the sociology of work, the future of work, human resource management, or environmental sustainability Experience in independent research methods such as interviews, surveys, or case studies Ability to collaborate with interdisciplinary research teams Candidates who are passionate about sustainability and interested in the intersection between environmental change and workplace culture are particularly encouraged to apply. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Application Deadline Application Deadline: April 20, 2026 Prospective applicants are encouraged to review the full project description and application requirements before submitting their materials. You can find these on the Find a PhD website. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Apply for the Fully Funded PhD on Environmental Change Agents in Hybrid and Remote Work Environments (UK) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: European Defence Agency Opens Applications for 2026 Traineeship Programme (Monthly stipend available)
summary: The European Defence Agency (EDA) has announced the opening of applications for its prestigious one-year paid Traineeship Programme, set to commence in September 2026. This initiative offers 20 positions across various specialized fields, including Cyber, Space, Industry, Communications, and more. An Opportunity for Recent Graduates Designed for recent university graduates, the EDA Traineeship Programme provides a unique gateway into the world of European defence cooperation. Participants will gain first-hand experience in a dynamic, multicultural professional environment at the heart of EU defence policy-making. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Key Benefits of the Traineeship Insight into European Defence Policies: Trainees will develop a deeper understanding of European defence capabilities and strategies. Application of Academic Knowledge: The programme allows participants to apply their academic background in a professional setting. International Collaboration: Trainees will work within an international team, fostering cross-border cooperation and professional growth. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the traineeship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Nationality: Must be a citizen of one of the Member States participating in the EDA. Education: Candidates must have completed at least a three-year cycle of higher education (university degree or equivalent) by the application deadline. Languages: A strong command of at least two languages from participating Member States, including English, is required. Relevant Background: The applicant s academic or professional background must align with the activities of the Directorate/Unit for which they apply. Personnel Security Clearance Certificate (PSCC): A clearance may be required for specific roles. Previous Experience: Candidates who have previously completed a paid or unpaid traineeship exceeding six weeks in another European institution or body are not eligible. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Traineeship Details Start Date: September 2025 (beginning either on the 1st or 16th of the month). Duration: 12 months, non-renewable. Allowance: Monthly stipend of 1,500 Meal Allowance: Fixed monthly amount. Support for Disabled Trainees: Additional financial support, up to 50% of the allowance, may be granted to cover extra costs related to disabilities. Insurance: Health and accident insurance is mandatory. Application Process Deadline: The deadline for applications is 16 April 2026 (depending on the position being applied for). Selection: Applicants can apply for a maximum of two positions, specifying their preference in a motivation letter. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Apply HERE Available Traineeship Positions Among the 20 available traineeships, positions include: Cyber Space Industry Communications Legal Affairs Finance Strategic Industrial Analysis Innovation Management EU-funded Defence Research Support Permanent Structured Cooperation (PESCO) Security Policy and Awareness Defence Standardisation, and more. Apply HERE About the European Defence Agency The European Defence Agency plays a pivotal role in enhancing European defence cooperation, fostering research and innovation, and supporting EU Member States in developing joint defence capabilities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The post European Defence Agency Opens Applications for 2026 Traineeship Programme (Monthly stipend available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youth Empowerment Fund 2026: Grants Up to €5,000 for Youth-Led SDG Projects (Apply by March 31)
summary: Applications are now open for the Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) 2026 , a global funding opportunity providing direct financial support to youth-led initiatives advancing the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) at the local level. The Youth Empowerment Fund is a partnership between the European Union (EU) and the Global Youth Mobilization (GYM) , an initiative of the Big Six Youth Organisations . The fund aims to empower young people to design and implement innovative solutions addressing social, environmental, and economic challenges in their communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Young people, informal youth groups, and youth-led organisations can apply for micro-grants ranging from 500 to 5,000 to support projects that create meaningful local impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Youth Empowerment Fund 2026 Overview Funding Provider: European Union Global Youth Mobilization Eligible Applicants: Youth aged 14 30 Funding Amount: 500 5,000 Eligible Applicants: Individuals, informal youth groups, and registered youth-led organisations Project Focus: Local initiatives aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 (23:59 UTC) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Youth Empowerment Fund Grants The Youth Empowerment Fund provides flexible funding depending on the type of applicant. Grants for Individuals and Informal Groups Young individuals or informal groups can apply for micro-grants between 500 and 1,500 to support small-scale community projects. Grants for Registered Youth-Led Organisations Registered youth-led organisations can apply for grants of up to 5,000 to implement larger initiatives that address community challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); These grants are designed to help young people turn innovative ideas into real solutions within their communities. Priority Areas for Youth Empowerment Fund Projects The Youth Empowerment Fund prioritizes initiatives that support vulnerable communities and promote sustainable development. Supporting Under-Represented Communities Projects that support marginalized groups are strongly encouraged, including initiatives that address: Socio-economic inequality Gender barriers and discrimination Disability inclusion Communities affected by climate change Youth facing marginalization or exclusion ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Climate Action and Environmental Sustainability The fund also supports projects addressing environmental challenges such as: Climate change adaptation and mitigation Environmental protection Sustainable community development Climate education and awareness Projects should demonstrate clear community impact and alignment with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Additional Opportunities for Selected Projects In addition to financial support, successful applicants will gain access to several development opportunities. Capacity Building Workshops Selected projects receive access to training opportunities including: Project management training Implementation guidance Networking with youth leaders and experts Global Youth Network Participants will also gain access to a global community of youth innovators and changemakers , providing opportunities to collaborate and share knowledge. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Accelerator Program Outstanding projects may be selected for the Youth Empowerment Fund Accelerator Program , which provides: Additional funding opportunities Advanced training and mentorship Access to global youth leadership networks Support to scale successful initiatives Youth-Led Selection Process Applications to the Youth Empowerment Fund are reviewed through the Global Youth Mobilization s youth-led model . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); A diverse international panel of youth reviewers evaluates applications and recommends projects for funding. This ensures that young people play a direct role in decision-making and resource allocation. Who Can Apply? To be eligible for the Youth Empowerment Fund, applicants must meet the following criteria: Age Requirement Applicants must be between 14 and 30 years old . Eligible Applicant Categories Applicants may apply as: Individual young people Informal youth groups Registered youth-led organisations How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their applications online through the official platform. Apply here: https://yef-gym.tfaforms.net/f/cycle3 For more info please visit: https://globalyouthmobilization.org/youth-led-solutions-open-call/ Eligible Projects Projects must: Address community challenges aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Demonstrate clear local impact Be led or co-led by young people Accessibility and Application Support The Youth Empowerment Fund is designed to ensure that the application process is accessible to all young people. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); The application platform: Is available in Arabic, English, French, and Spanish Allows applicants to include video submissions or supporting materials Offers offline forms and accommodations for applicants facing internet or accessibility challenges Applicants requiring support can contact: EU-YEF@globalyouthmobilization.org Why You Should Apply The Youth Empowerment Fund offers young leaders the opportunity to: Access funding for community initiatives Address local challenges aligned with global development goals Build leadership and project management skills Join a global network of youth changemakers For many young people and grassroots youth organisations, this fund represents an opportunity to transform ideas into real community impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Youth Empowerment Fund 2026 Deadline The deadline to apply for the Youth Empowerment Fund is: 31 March 2026 (23:59 UTC). Early applications are encouraged. How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their applications online through the official platform. Apply here: https://yef-gym.tfaforms.net/f/cycle3 For more info please visit: https://globalyouthmobilization.org/youth-led-solutions-open-call/ The post Youth Empowerment Fund 2026: Grants Up to €5,000 for Youth-Led SDG Projects (Apply by March 31) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Luxembourg Working Student Program at JPMorganChase | 2026 Virtual Event
summary: The JPMorgan Chase Global Career Event is a prestigious professional networking and recruitment opportunity organized by one of the world s leading financial institutions, JPMorgan Chase Co. . These events are designed to introduce talented students and emerging professionals to the company s career opportunities while providing insider knowledge about the financial services industry. Through this event, participants gain valuable insights into the company s businesses, culture, and recruitment process while interacting directly with recruiters, industry professionals, and recent graduates working at the firm. The event also serves as a gateway to internships, graduate programs, and full-time employment opportunities within the organization. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); As a global leader in investment banking, financial services, and asset management, JPMorgan Chase actively seeks the best talent worldwide to support its operations and innovation across markets. The company operates globally and serves millions of customers and institutional clients through its broad range of financial products and services. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Overview of the JPMorgan Career Networking Event This event is a professional conference and recruitment event designed to connect potential candidates with JPMorgan s global teams. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants who attend the event will gain: Insight into the company s core businesses and global operations Career guidance from JPMorgan professionals Networking opportunities with recruiters and employees Information about internship programs and graduate recruitment Tips on application processes, interviews, and career preparation These sessions are often hosted virtually or at selected global locations and may include presentations, panel discussions, Q A sessions, and networking opportunities with company representatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Such recruitment events play a major role in helping candidates understand how their skills, interests, and academic backgrounds can translate into a career in finance, technology, consulting, or business operations. About JPMorgan Chase Co. JPMorgan Chase Co. is one of the largest and most influential financial services companies in the world. Headquartered in New York, the firm provides a wide range of services including: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Investment banking Consumer banking Commercial banking Asset and wealth management Financial transaction processing The company manages trillions of dollars in assets and serves corporations, governments, and individuals across the globe. With extensive global operations and a strong reputation in finance, JPMorgan Chase offers career opportunities in numerous sectors including technology, finance, risk management, data science, and consulting. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); For aspiring professionals, participating in a JPMorgan career event provides direct exposure to one of the most respected employers in the financial industry. What Participants Will Learn at the Event Attending the JPMorgan career event allows participants to explore several important topics related to career development and the global financial industry. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 1. Understanding JPMorgan s Global Business Lines Participants will learn about major divisions within the company such as: Corporate Investment Banking Global Markets Asset Wealth Management Consumer Community Banking Technology and Digital Innovation These sessions help candidates understand the company s structure and how different teams contribute to global financial markets. 2. Career Opportunities at JPMorgan The event will highlight several entry points into the company, including: Summer internships Graduate programs Full-time analyst roles Early-career rotational programs Recruiters often provide guidance on how applicants can transition from university into competitive financial careers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); 3. Recruitment Process Insights One of the most valuable aspects of this event is the opportunity to learn about the company s recruitment process. Participants will receive guidance on: Preparing strong resumes and cover letters Navigating online applications Succeeding in technical and behavioral interviews Developing networking strategies Understanding these processes significantly increases applicants chances of success when applying to JPMorgan programs. 4. Networking with Industry Professionals Attendees will also have the opportunity to interact directly with: JPMorgan recruiters Recent graduates working at the firm Senior professionals in banking and finance ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Networking sessions allow participants to ask questions about career paths, workplace culture, and skill development. Who Can Apply for the JPMorgan Career Event This opportunity is designed primarily for students and early-career professionals. title: New York Women’s Foundation Spring 2026 Early Investment Grant
summary: The Spring 2026 Early Investment Grant offered by The New York Women s Foundation is a strategic funding opportunity designed to support grassroots organizations and community-based initiatives working to advance gender, racial, and economic justice. This grant program is part of the foundation s long-standing commitment to investing in innovative, community-driven solutions that uplift women, girls, and gender-expansive individuals. Founded in 1987, the foundation has become one of the leading women-led philanthropic institutions globally, focusing on funding early-stage organizations and initiatives that address systemic inequalities affecting marginalized communities. The Early Investment Grant specifically targets emerging or under-resourced organizations whose work promotes social change and empowerment. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This funding opportunity is particularly valuable because it provides not only financial support but also credibility and long-term partnership opportunities. Many organizations funded by the foundation go on to receive additional investments and scale their impact significantly. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Overview of the Spring 2026 Early Investment Grant Program The Spring 2026 Early Investment Grant is designed to fund organizations that are at an early stage of development but demonstrate strong potential to create transformative change within their communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The program emphasizes participatory grantmaking. This means that funding decisions are guided by community members, activists, and individuals with lived experiences related to the issues being addressed. This model ensures that resources are directed to the initiatives most capable of generating meaningful impact. Through this approach, the foundation aims to: Support grassroots leadership Empower communities affected by inequality Promote gender justice and economic equity Build sustainable and scalable solutions Strengthen local organizations serving marginalized populations ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Organizations selected for the grant will receive funding for a one-year period and may also gain access to networking opportunities, capacity-building resources, and collaborative partnerships. Grant Timeline and Key Dates Applicants must carefully observe the timeline to ensure they do not miss important stages in the application process. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Important Dates for the Spring 2026 Grant Cycle Information Session: March 5, 2026 (2:00 PM EST) Application Deadline: 23rd March 2026 Site Visit Invitations Sent: 3rd April 2026 Site Visit Period: 13th April 1st May 2026 Funding Decision Notification: 1st June 2026 Grant Implementation Period: 1st July 2026 30th June 2027 Applicants are strongly encouraged to participate in the information session to better understand the program requirements and evaluation criteria. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Who Can Apply for the NYWF Spring 2026 Grant The grant is specifically designed for organizations that serve communities experiencing economic, racial, and gender-based inequalities. Eligible applicants include: Grassroots nonprofit organizations Community-based organizations Advocacy groups Social justice initiatives Women-led and gender-inclusive organizations Emerging organizations addressing community challenges ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The foundation prioritizes organizations that work directly with: Women and girls Gender-expansive individuals Immigrants LGBTQI+ communities Low-income families People with disabilities More than 95% of the foundation s funding supports organizations serving low- to moderate-income communities, reflecting its commitment to equity and inclusion. Geographic Eligibility: Countries or Regions That Can Apply This opportunity has a specific geographic focus. Eligible applicants must: Operate within the United States Serve communities located within the five boroughs of New York City ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); These boroughs include: Manhattan Brooklyn Queens The Bronx Staten Island Organizations outside these areas are generally not eligible unless their work directly impacts communities within these boroughs. Organizational Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Early Investment Grant, organizations must meet several basic criteria. Legal Status Requirements Applicants must: Be a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, or Have a 501(c)(3) fiscal sponsor This requirement ensures that funded organizations comply with nonprofit regulations and can properly manage grant funds. Organizational Development Stage The program particularly supports emerging or early-stage organizations, including those that: Have limited access to institutional funding Are developing innovative solutions to community challenges Are led by individuals with lived experience of the issues they address ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The foundation frequently acts as one of the first institutional investors in the organizations it funds, helping them establish credibility and attract additional funding sources. Key Focus Areas of the Grant The Spring 2026 Early Investment Grant prioritizes initiatives addressing critical social issues affecting women and marginalized communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Key focus areas include: 1. Health Equity and Reproductive Justice Programs that expand healthcare access, reproductive rights, and health education for women and underserved populations. 2. Economic Justice Initiatives aimed at improving financial stability, employment opportunities, and economic empowerment. 3. Safety and Healing Justice Projects that address gender-based violence, trauma recovery, and community safety. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); 4. Expanding Democracy and Leadership Programs that increase civic engagement, leadership development, and representation of marginalized groups. What the Grant Supports Funding from the Early Investment Grant can be used for a wide range of activities that strengthen organizations and their community programs. These may include: Launching new programs Expanding existing initiatives Community organizing and advocacy Capacity-building and operational support Leadership development Outreach and community engagement Strategic planning and program development The foundation s approach focuses on long-term sustainability, ensuring that funded organizations have the resources to continue their work beyond the grant period. The Application Process Applying for the Spring 2026 Early Investment Grant involves several steps designed to evaluate the organization s impact and potential. Step 1: Attend the Information Session Organizations are encouraged to attend the March 5 information session to learn about the program guidelines and application process. Step 2: Prepare the Application Applicants must submit their proposals through the foundation s online grant management platform. Typical application materials may include: Organizational background Mission and program description Community impact statement Budget and funding plan Leadership and governance details Step 3: Application Submission All applications must be submitted before the March 23, 2026 deadline. Late submissions are typically not accepted. Step 4: Site Visit Selected organizations may be invited for a site visit between April 13 and May 1, 2026. These visits allow the foundation to learn more about the organization s work and community impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Step 5: Final Decision Successful applicants will receive official funding notifications by June 1, 2026. Apply here Benefits of Receiving the NYWF Early Investment Grant Organizations that receive funding gain several advantages beyond financial support. 1. Financial Investment Funding helps organizations expand programs and implement impactful initiatives. 2. Institutional Credibility Being funded by a respected philanthropic organization strengthens an organization s reputation and fundraising capacity. 3. Network and Partnerships Grantees join a network of community leaders, activists, and organizations working toward shared goals. 4. Capacity Building Grantees may receive training, mentorship, and strategic support that improves long-term organizational sustainability. Why the New York Women s Foundation Supports Early-Stage Organizations The philosophy behind the Early Investment Grant is that community-led solutions are the most effective drivers of social change. The foundation believes that people directly affected by social problems are best positioned to design solutions. By funding grassroots organizations early in their development, the foundation helps these initiatives grow into influential and sustainable movements. This approach reflects a broader commitment to participatory philanthropy, where communities play a central role in decision-making about funding priorities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); Deadline Applicants must ensure they submit their applications before the official deadline. 23rd March 2026 The Spring 2026 Early Investment Grant by the New York Women s Foundation represents an important funding opportunity for organizations committed to advancing gender equity, racial justice, and economic empowerment. By investing in emerging grassroots organizations, the foundation continues to strengthen community leadership and drive meaningful social transformation. For organizations working to empower women, girls, and marginalized communities in New York City, this grant provides a powerful platform for growth, sustainability, and long-term impact. title: UNAOC -BMW Group Intercultural Innovation Hub 2026: Global Funding Opportunity for Non-Profit Organizations Advancing Social Inclusion and Intercultural Dialogue (Up to $20,000 Grant)
summary: Organizations around the world working to promote intercultural dialogue, social inclusion, and community cohesion now have a major opportunity to expand their impact. The Intercultural Innovation Hub 2026 has officially opened its global call for applications, inviting non-profit organizations to apply for financial support, mentorship, and capacity-building opportunities that can help scale their innovative social initiatives. The program is a collaboration between the United Nations Alliance of Civilizations, BMW Group, and Accenture, bringing together global expertise from international organizations, the private sector, and civil society. Together, these partners aim to strengthen innovative projects that foster dialogue among cultures, tackle prejudice, and build inclusive societies worldwide. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); If your organization is working to address social tensions, promote diversity, or use innovative methods to foster understanding across communities, this program could provide the resources and global network needed to amplify your impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); What Is the Intercultural Innovation Hub? The Intercultural Innovation Hub (IIH) is a global initiative designed to identify and support innovative civil society projects that promote dialogue and cooperation between cultures. The program focuses on scaling social innovations that contribute to more inclusive societies and help address global challenges related to cultural misunderstanding and social division. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The hub acts as a platform for collaboration, funding, and capacity building. It brings together social innovators, non-profit organizations, and global experts to develop scalable solutions to complex intercultural issues. Since its creation, the program has supported organizations that tackle topics such as discrimination, extremism, inequality, and cultural exclusion. Selected organizations gain access not only to financial resources but also to strategic mentoring and an international network of changemakers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Funding and Benefits for Selected Organizations Organizations selected for the Intercultural Innovation Hub will receive a comprehensive package designed to help them expand and strengthen their projects. 1. Financial Grant Each finalist organization will receive up to USD $20,000 to support the expansion of its social innovation project. The funding is specifically intended to scale initiatives and increase their impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); This financial support can help organizations: Expand programs into new communities or regions Strengthen project implementation Increase beneficiary outreach Improve sustainability and long-term impact However, the funding is not intended for general operational costs such as salaries or office rent. Instead, it supports activities directly related to expanding the selected project. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 2. One-Year Capacity-Building Program Selected organizations participate in a year-long development program that includes: Workshops and masterclasses Mentorship from experts in innovation and social impact Strategic guidance for scaling projects Customized organizational support These training sessions are designed to strengthen leadership skills, project management capacity, and innovation strategies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 3. Access to a Global Network Another major advantage of the Intercultural Innovation Hub is membership in a global community of changemakers. Organizations will gain opportunities to: Connect with global experts and partners Participate in international events and workshops Collaborate with other organizations tackling similar challenges Share knowledge and best practices across regions This network helps organizations build partnerships that extend far beyond the grant period. Key Focus Areas for Projects The program is looking for innovative projects already making an impact in communities. Projects should address intercultural challenges and promote inclusion. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Examples of eligible focus areas include: 1. Combating Xenophobia and Hate Speech Projects addressing discrimination, racism, and hate speech through education, advocacy, or digital tools. 2. Preventing Violent Extremism Initiatives designed to counter radicalization and promote peaceful dialogue across communities. 3. Promoting Gender Equality Programs empowering women and promoting equal participation in society. 4. Social Inclusion Through Arts, Sports, and Culture Projects using creative or cultural activities to build bridges between communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); 5. Building Inclusive and Cohesive Societies Initiatives strengthening community resilience and collaboration across diverse populations. 6. Using Technology for Social Good Innovative uses of technology, including artificial intelligence, to promote intercultural understanding and human well-being. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Who Can Apply? The Intercultural Innovation Hub is open to non-profit organizations worldwide that are already implementing projects related to intercultural dialogue and inclusion. Eligible Applicants Organizations that may apply include: Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Charitable organizations Research or educational institutions Faith-based organizations Professional associations Foundations Indigenous organizations Labor unions Local or municipal government branches Applications are not open to individuals. Applicants must apply through a registered non-profit organization. Eligible Countries and Regions This opportunity is open globally, meaning organizations from any continent or country can apply. Eligible regions include: Africa Asia Europe North and South America Oceania The program specifically encourages diverse participation to ensure global representation of intercultural initiatives. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Intercultural Innovation Hub 2026, organizations must meet several criteria. Organizational Requirements Applicants must: Be registered as a non-profit organization. Have been operational for at least two years prior to the application period. Demonstrate a clear track record of implementing social impact projects. Show commitment to diversity, inclusion, and intercultural dialogue. Project Requirements Projects submitted must: Already be actively implemented and not just an idea. Have demonstrated measurable impact during at least one year of operation. Show potential for expansion or replication in other contexts. Focus on intercultural dialogue and inclusive societies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Projects that consist only of one-time events such as conferences or festivals are not eligible. Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on three major categories: 1. Project Quality Reviewers will assess: Relevance to social challenges Innovation and originality Sustainability of the approach Measurable outcomes and impact Potential for replication in other contexts 2. Organizational Strength Evaluation includes: Organizational structure and leadership Transparency and accountability Commitment to equality and diversity Realistic project work plan and budget 3. Application Quality Applications must clearly communicate: Project objectives Impact strategy Implementation plan Expected results and scalability Organizations must present a compelling narrative and clear evidence of impact to increase their chances of selection. Application Process Organizations must apply online through the Intercultural Innovation Hub application platform. The process typically includes the following steps: Submit an online application form. Eligibility screening by program organizers. Shortlisting of approximately 30 applicants. Final selection of 10 organizations by an international jury. Finalists will then be invited to join the Intercultural Innovation Hub program and participate in the capacity-building activities. Apply here Why Organizations Should Apply The Intercultural Innovation Hub offers far more than financial support. It provides a unique opportunity for social impact organizations to scale their work internationally. Benefits include: Access to funding for project expansion Strategic mentorship from global experts Capacity-building training Visibility and recognition on an international stage Opportunities to collaborate with global changemakers For organizations dedicated to building inclusive societies and addressing cultural divisions, the program offers both resources and global exposure. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Deadline 15th April 2026 at 5:00 p.m. EDT (New York time) . Late submissions will not be accepted. The Intercultural Innovation Hub 2026 represents a powerful opportunity for non-profit organizations around the world to strengthen their social innovation initiatives. By combining funding, mentorship, and global collaboration, the program aims to accelerate impactful solutions that promote dialogue, inclusion, and mutual understanding across cultures.
